items.subject: Action Needed: Upgrade the Security of Your Salesforce Experience
items.body_text:
[Salesforce]
Product & Service Notification

As part of our ongoing efforts to support the security of our customers in light of the evolving cybersecurity threats we face, Salesforce is planning additional enhancements to Salesforce orgs and tenants. The upcoming changes are designed to enforce more stringent security controls, and will fall under these categories: 
Strengthened User Identity: Protect all users by requiring MFA and phishing-resistant MFA
Enhanced Data Protection: Additional verification for sensitive operations such as bulk data exports
Adaptive Access Controls: Risk-based verification to ensure only authorized users and devices access your data

Immediate Action Required: Email Domain Verification 
 
To better protect customers against email spoofing and phishing attacks, email domain verification is now required to send emails from Salesforce. Admins must verify ownership of email-sending domains to avoid disruption in email delivery by these dates: 
New domains in all orgs: Starting March 24, 2026 (with the Spring ’26, patch 11 release)
Recently-used domains in sandboxes: April 7, 2026
Recently-used domains in production and all other orgs: April 27, 2026Verify your domains by activating a DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) key or by verifying an Authorized Email Domain. If verification is not possible, an option to use a substitute domain is also available. Learn more about the change and timeline. 
New Security Control Requirements Beginning June 2026 
 
Beginning June 2026, Salesforce plans to require the implementation of additional security controls and settings. To ensure a seamless transition and maintain your organization's security posture, we recommend that you take the following actions now (even if previously configured) and confirm your settings meet these requirements: 
Require Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): Ensure MFA is required in sandbox and production orgs, for all employee license users, either through Salesforce or through an SSO provider. Even with MFA enabled for login, certain sensitive actions after login will trigger step-up authentication. For help with implementing MFA, see Multi-Factor Authentication for Salesforce Orgs.
Ensure all System Administrator users adopt Phishing-Resistant MFA for login: Phishing-Resistant MFA requires built-in authenticators, security keys, or equivalent. To make phishing-resistant MFA options available to users, enable built-in authenticators or security keys.
Restrict Login IP Addresses in Profiles: Specifying allowed IP address ranges on profiles denies a user access if they attempt to sign in from an unauthorized IP address. Note that by default, this check applies at login time only and users are not automatically logged out mid-session if their IP address changes. To enforce IP range validation on every request (not just at login), "Enforce login IP ranges on every request" must be enabled in Session Settings. Only when this setting is active will users be logged out mid-session due to an IP address change. This additional protection is particularly important if your org has not implemented Phishing-Resistant MFA. See Restrict Login IP Addresses.
Enable a Transaction Security Policy (TSP) that Restricts Large Data Exports: It has been previously recommended that Salesforce Shield and Event Monitoring customers have a TSP on ReportEvent that triggers step-up authentication when report data is downloaded. In June, 2026, if an org with Shield or Event Monitoring does not already have one of these TSPs in place, one will be added and enabled automatically. See Transaction Security .
Avoid Connecting from Anonymizing Proxies and High-Risk IP Addresses: Ensure your users are not connecting to Salesforce via anonymizing VPNs or from other high-risk IP addresses. Salesforce monitors for and blocks high-risk connections and will continue to do so.We will be announcing a roadmap of additional security control requirements and timelines in the near future. We understand that our customers need time to make these adjustments, and will do our best to ensure these changes are communicated in a timely manner. 

© 2026 Salesforce, Inc. 
Salesforce Tower, 415 Mission Street, 3rd Floor, San Francisco, CA 94105, United States 
General Inquiries: 415-901-7000 | 1-800-667-6389 
This email was sent to adam.nelson@aptum.com 
Privacy Statement

HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.
 
Kindly revert on any clarifications.
 

Thanks & Regards
 
Rupesh Sankar
Business Development Manager

Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace
 
Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS? 
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?
If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer… 
Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor? 
 
 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

We operate as one unified team, and Gina is instrumental in driving our overall strategic direction and leadership initiatives. Instead, we have a group of dedicated specialists who are aligned with specific focus areas, such as Migration, PPA, and other AWS programs. These team members are equipped to fully support all your requirements and provide hands-on assistance.
 
Whenever your project requires strategic alignment or broader insights, we can absolutely involve Gina or other key stakeholders in the conversation. At the same time, you’ll have the right team members focused on day-to-day execution to ensure everything runs smoothly.
 
Please rest assured you’ll receive strong, reliable support from our side, and we’re committed to making this partnership a success.

AWS Distribution Partnership Discussion
Date: Recent

Executive Summary
Aptum is evaluating moving their AWS distribution partnership to TDS to address customer visibility concerns and improve margin structure. Key opportunity: $2M from a $5M AWS customer account, targeting 6% margins with 3% customer pass-through.
 
Key Discussion Points
Current Situation
Customer can see aggregator discounts via IAM access → causing dissatisfaction
Current margin: 4% from existing distributor
Customer considering going direct to AWS without better pricing
Decision needed: Move DSA to TDS before April 1st deadline
 
April 1st AWS Program Changes
Major structural changes:
Partners receive full 8% front-end discount (up from 3%)
Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)
New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor
TDS Model:
100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"
No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts
Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now
Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration
Current spend: $200K+/month direct with AWS
Aptum led migration from $0 → $50K+/month using MAP funding
Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions
Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

Complete due diligence with other distributors
TDS (@Rupesh):

Important Notes
Timing Considerations:
April 1st program changes happen regardless of distributor choice
DSA transfer can complete within hours (pending agreement terms)
90-day notification clause could delay April 1st implementation
Partner Growth Incentive lost for one year when switching distributors
Current Limitations:
AWS hasn't released final program documentation (expected in "couple more weeks")

Aptum completes distributor evaluation

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 

 

Thanks,

Michael

HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.
 
Kindly revert on any clarifications.

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS? 
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?
If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer… 
Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor?

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 

 

Thanks,

Michael

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS? 
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?

Partner Growth Incentive lost for one year when switching distributors

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 

 

Thanks,

Michael

HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 

 

Thanks,

Michael

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Michael

---

[Matthew Carter]
Hi David Blankinship, apologies, this is on my To Do list for today: I agree someone should connect with Basis, but to ensure we're on the same page, we've missed the window (I believe) to move over to TDSynnex for April 1, but Ingram will still honour the 8% discount from AWS. The narrative to Basis should be that they will receive the discount starting in April (i.e., on May's invoice) and we are still transitioning away from Ingram to another aggregator in the new few weeks.

---

[David Blankinship]
Hello all- It maybe a wise decision for someone to reach out to Andy from Basis about this. He has been promised and today for months by the Aptum team that this would be done by  April 1st. If you know its not going to be completed, it would be best to let him know asap rather than the day of or day before as he was told in February that this was still in alignment with the April 1st date. 

I also, would let Ian know this as there I'm sure will be fall back on the Basis side with Andy and the Michael F. unfortunately

items.subject: Upcoming limits for all Jira apps
items.body_text:
[Atlassian]
New Jira limits, guardrails, and data mgt. tools are coming

 

Accelerating Jira for all teams 
Announcing expanded limits, guardrails, and data management tools 
Hello, 
We're reaching out to inform you about upcoming changes to the Jira Cloud apps: Jira, Jira Service Management, and Jira Product Discovery. 
Starting in September 2026, Atlassian will enforce several new Jira limits, listed here. We are now also rolling out enforcement on previously announced limits. 

• New limits: starting September 2026, the Jira Cloud family of apps will enforce new limits on certain data types. These limits are designed to prevent performance slowdowns and ensure a smoother user experience. 

• New guardrails: we've released new guardrails, which are recommended thresholds for data volume and configuration. These best practices help you keep your sites healthy and high‑performing. 

• Enhanced data management tools: we're rolling out new data cleanup tools, available to all customers, to help you find and easily manage unused or outdated data. 

◦ Optimize work item security schemes 

◦ Optimize field options 
These limits will not block migrating cloud customers; all migrating customers will have 6 months from the date of their migration to adhere to Jira Cloud limits. 
With these clear limits and simplified data cleanup tools, admins can more easily manage data, improve site health, and keep Jira running reliably for their teams. 

Learn more 
Thanks for partnering with us as we continue to optimize Jira for everyone. 

Read in browser • Privacy policy • Contact us • Read our blog • Join Atlassian Community 
     
Copyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved. We are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia 
 
You are receiving this email because this is an important message regarding your account and apps you are using. You are not allowed to unsubscribe from this type of message.

Brookfield Asset Management, La Caisse to Take Boralex Private 

 

Brookfield Asset Management and La Caisse have agreed to take Canadian renewable-energy major Boralex private in a deal with an implied equity value of approximately $3.8 billion.
 
La Caisse, currently Boralex’s largest shareholder with roughly 15% ownership, has agreed to support the transaction and will reinvest post-closing to hold a 30% stake in the privatized company, with Brookfield acquiring the remaining 70%.
 
Read more ...

Get Connect Daily 

Submit a Press Release

[Frederic Gingras]
OUTPUT 1: THE CUSTOMER EMAIL

Subject: Quick recap from our call

Hi Brad,

Thanks again for making the time today. I appreciated the straight talk and the context on where things stand as you normalize the IT function and set up for the next phase.

From what I understand, Vergent LMS, Inc. has come a long way from the period last fall when the team was stretched thin and you needed extra coverage to catch up on the basics that were starting to create noise with auditors and internally. With Aptum involved, you were able to stabilize, get patching and operational hygiene back under control, and build a steadier day to day rhythm while your internal team got its footing again. [Vergent LM...ation Chat | Event], [office.com]

What is making this harder now is that the environment and the people around it are changing at the same time. Andrew is leaving at the end of the month, a new Andrew is joining full time, and you are also in the middle of removing a meaningful amount of lower environment footprint where round the clock coverage no longer feels justified. Production still needs immediate support when it matters, but lower and dev issues can be handled by your internal team during business hours with the alerting you already have in place, and that shift naturally creates pressure to realign scope and cost so the contract reflects the new reality as endpoints and monitoring come out. [Vergent LM...ation Chat | Event], [Vergent x...stems chat | Event]

The turning point I heard is that you are trying to normalize now, not later, so you can stop paying for a temporary posture and put that spend toward growth. The modernization work is moving forward in parallel, with lower environments transitioning away from traditional virtual machines toward managed web applications, and databases moving off self managed virtual machines toward managed MariaDB services in Azure. That creates a clear moment to step back and ask what level of support you actually need going into the next contract term, especially with the new Andrew taking ownership of the decision once he is in seat and has had time to work with everyone. [Vergent x...stems chat | Event], [Vergent LM...ation Chat | Event], [Vergent -...arch 2026) | Word]

If this plays out the way you described, success looks like a support model and commercial structure that tracks to what is truly mission critical, protects production, and still keeps you confident going into a growth year. In the near term, that means we land the warm handover between the outgoing and incoming Andrew, reconcile the lower environment endpoint count so we are all working from the same baseline, and align on the database migration glidepath through your October target while acknowledging some production databases may remain due to existing customer commitments. From there, we can translate the drawdown into a clean adjustment to scope and billing, so you are not funding yesterday’s footprint while you focus on onboarding new clients and scaling the right parts of the platform. [Vergent LM...ation Chat | Event], [Vergent -...arch 2026) | Word]

If I missed the nuance on which lower environment items still need any light touch support, or how you want us to treat web apps versus virtual machines in the endpoint math, please correct me. I would rather get the story right now so our delivery teams step into the next conversation with the right context.

Talk soon,

Your Name] OUTPUT 2: CONFIDENCE & GAPS ASSESSMENT     CONFIDENCE & GAPS     =================     Email metadata       No gaps identified for customer first name, interaction type, or sender placeholder     Setting:       🟢       → Grounded in the call transcript and existing Vergent context     Complication:  🟢       → Clear drivers: people transition, scope drawdown in lower environments, need to realign service levels and cost     Turning Point: 🟡       → I inferred the core catalyst as “normalize spend now to fund growth” tied to the modernization push and leadership handoff, but I do not have a single explicit sentence that states the primary trigger in those exact words       Questions to validate         What was the biggest reason to revisit this now: budget pressure, contract timing around September, or operational confidence after stabilizing the team         Is the new Andrew expected to keep the same overall relationship but tune service levels, or is he considering a more material change in how support is structured     Resolution:    🟡       → Desired business outcomes are clear, but a few specifics remain ambiguous and should be confirmed before we speak with delivery teams .docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1)       Questions to validate         When you say “production still needs immediate support,” what does “good” look like in practice: on call coverage only for production, or also for a defined subset of shared services         For the databases, you mentioned an October target tied to the DB contract, but also that some will remain due to specific customer contracts. Which systems are in the “must remain” bucket, and what is the best current estimate of how many that is, since we heard a wide range         Commercially, would you prefer a clean mid term adjustment now, or a broader renegotiation that could also extend the end date, since you floated flexibility there If you want, I can also generate a second version of the email that is shorter and more executive in tone while keeping the same four paragraph narrative.

---

[Frederic Gingras]
updated prompt:

 

ou are an account manager writing a short customer story to send back to the customer for confirmation.

Your goal is NOT to summarize meeting notes.

Your goal is to demonstrate deep understanding of the customer’s situation, motivations, and underlying concerns. And get the customer confirmation.

 

Use the following storytelling arc inspired by Ben Zoldan Storyleader approach:

 

Setting: what the company does; what their success means; was there an M&A in play; highlite any proud moment getting where they are now (if applicable); can add how they are using us (if valuable to mention).

 

Complication: the pain; the problem that is encountered; what they are talking to us; This is not a need for a solution, this is a problem; it’s the why of the discussion.

 

Turning point: ah ha moment; what if we were doing this, how that would help; the realization.

 

Resolution: what an end state would look like; what ideal looks like.

 

Finish by asking the customer to confirm if we got this right.

 

Instructions: Write in clear, human language suitable for an email.

Do not list technologies unless they directly support the emotional or business tension.

Do not repeat facts verbatim from the notes.

Translate facts into intent, pressure, frustration, ambition, or risk.

Explicitly surface what feels uncomfortable, risky, or heavy for the customer.

If emotions are not stated directly, infer them cautiously and reasonably (as in “maybe this is causing you…”).

Assume the reader is the customer and ask yourself: “Would they say yes, that’s exactly it?”

 

Tone guidelines:

Curious

Respectful

Empathetic

Strategic

Not salesy

Not technical documentation

 

Length guidelines: 4 short paragraphs (as per story arc described earlier)

No bullet points if possible

No dashes in all cases

No section headers in the final output

End the story with a sentence that invites correction, such as: “Let me know where this resonates or where we missed the mark.”

 

Here are my raw meeting notes:

items.subject: ATL1 Network Maintenance 11th April 2026 9PM - 1AM ET
items.body_text:
[notification@thinkon.com]
Hello,

Thank you for being a loyal ThinkOn customer. Our team of Thinkers are hard at work to bring you the seamless and positive digital performance experience you expect from ThinkOn.

Outage Details:
Reason for Maintenance: To improve reliability, performance, and mitigate legacy equipment risks
Start Time: Saturday, April 11th, 2026, 9PM ET
End Time: Sunday, April 12th, 2026, 1AM ET
Expected Duration: 4hrs
Services Affected: Internet/Network access will be impacted during this maintenance window. Our team will work to ensure the maintenance is completed as quickly and smoothly as possible.

Site Impacted: ATL1 (Roswell Data Center)

If you have any questions, concerns, or comments about this service maintenance, please reach out to us at requests@thinkon.com or by calling 1-877-787-0306.

Thank you for your continued commitment to ThinkOn—our customers are our top priority. Together, we make data thrive.

ThinkOn Operations and Customer Experience
Toll free: 1-877-787-0306
requests@thinkon.com
items.body_snippet:

items.subject: Re: BI Report: Capture Pending Customer Approval “orders”
items.body_text:
[Jorge Quintero]
Thanks Lacie, comments below

Jorge Quintero
Director, Business Intelligence
+1 (305) 717-6674
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 11:34 AM
To: Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; SupplyChain-Ops <SupplyChain-Ops@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: BI Report: Capture Pending Customer Approval “orders” 

Good Afternoon, 

Initial feedback is that the report seems to be pulling orders which are also below the 30 day cut-off, are we able to amend the filter to exclude those? 
JQ - Yes, I can exclude those no problem.

Second feedback would be that it would be preferable for this to be actioned through a Jira ticket that should automatically be assigned to the SDM queue and not via an email distro. I do a review of the ticket queue with my team every Wednesday at 9am ET so if it could be sent before 8am to align with this it would be ideal. 
JQ - I'm guessing it will be one ticket with all the data orders at once, not a ticket per order, correct?
Also, there was a mechanism that allowed me to send an email to a specific email address and a Jira ticket would be created. Is that available?

Since there is a number of these aged orders (~30) I'd like to know when this is going to go into effect so that I can make my team aware, and put a plan in place to address this first influx. I have already confirmed that they all have the correct permissions to retrieve and cancel orders (I do not have these permissions). 

Thank you,

Lacie Allen-Morley
Manager, Service Delivery
+442380926163
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 20:27

add/delete/modify requirement to the attached data.

Jorge

aptum.com | LinkedIn

Subject: BI Report: Capture Pending Customer Approval “orders”
 
Hi Jorge,
Is it possible to create a BI report to capture pending customer approval “orders” that are idle in the mypeer1 portal greater than 30 days?

Thanks,

items.subject: Claude for Finance - Cash Forecasting & FP&A Basics (Practical AI Workflows for Finance Teams)
items.body_text:
[AI workflows for finance teams]
You don't often get email from reply@mail.virtualtrainings.co. Learn why this is important 

Learn how AI can simplify cash forecasting and streamline FP&A processes for your finance team.

To view this email as a web page, go here.

  

 

Live Virtual Class 

Claude for Finance - Cash Forecasting & FP&A Basics (Practical AI Workflows for Finance Teams) 

  

April 01, 2026 

12:00 PM - 01:30 PM (Eastern Time)
Duration: 1 Hour 30 Minutes
REGISTER NOW! 

Overview 
This 90-minute online session teaches practical AI foundations for finance professionals and a step-by-step approach to using Claude for core finance workflows. Participants learn how to structure prompts for forecasting and analysis, turn raw inputs into clear narratives, and validate Claude outputs before they reach leaders or stakeholders. Course Objective:

AI foundations for finance: where Claude helps vs. where it’s risky. 
Prompt frameworks for finance outputs (templates, constraints, reusable formats). 
Cash forecasting workflow support (structure, assumptions, scenarios). 

Read more... 

If you do not wish to receive further emails from us, Please click here to Unsubscribe here 

This email was sent to: anelson@cloudops.com 
This email was sent by: VirtualTrainings
39658 Mission Boulevard Fremont, CA 94539 USA 
We respect your right to privacy - view our policy

 

Manage Subscriptions | Update Profile | Unsubscribe

Thanks,
Sharon Wilton
 
From: Sharon Wilton
Sent: Tuesday, 24 March 2026
To: 'adam.nelson@aptum.com' <adam.nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: AWS Users
 
Hi Adam,

Looking to connect with decision-makers at companies using AWS Solutions?

Share your target products, and we’ll send you the relevant counts and more details.

Regards,
Sharon Wilton

Nedim

items.subject: Zendesk email automation
items.body_text:
[Sam Cooper]
You don't often get email from sam.cooper@giosgzen.com. Learn why this is important 

Curious if you've automated your support inbox yet?‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­‌   ­ 

Hi Adam,

Is your support team still writing out every email reply manually, or have you found a way to automate that yet?

We've been helping teams with auto-drafted emails based on their past tickets and real company data so they only have to review them before sending.

Is this worth a 5-minute chat given your volume, or is everything under control on your end?

Best,
Sam Cooper
giosg.com

Unsubscribe
